steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The previous sentence establishes that scholars hold a balanced view: they affirm the model's economic sustainability while acknowledging its challenges.

The following sentence then describes people who depart from this balance—extremists on both sides who ignore one side of the evidence.

The key signal is the contrast between the nuanced scholars and those who lack this nuance.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
We need a transition that acknowledges this contrast: yes, scholars are balanced, but others are not. "That said" signals exactly this shift—introducing a complicating or contrasting point.

The phrase "some experts overlook such nuance" directly connects to the balanced view just described and names the problem: some (unlike the many scholars mentioned) lack this balance.

The examples that follow demonstrate how: those declaring the model unviable discount successes; those championing it understate challenges.

Option D reverses the scholars' position (they are not skeptical), option C introduces solutions out of nowhere, and option B pivots to an unrelated claim about overcoming challenges being contentious.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
